what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel memory attachment that links an attachment ID to an image asset target in the design-assets channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Uses a custom image:// URI scheme for the target
Leaves the body empty after the frontmatter
concerns: The target URI cannot be verified or resolved from this file alone
No author, alt text, dimensions, or checksum are recorded
attachment_type uses a plural label without further specificity
notes: A minimal ledger entry that registers an image attachment pointing to the Price_Icon asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does the Price_Icon image depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the image://design-assets/icons/Price_Icon target currently valid?
How does this attachment relate to other attachments or messages in the channel?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the fields indicate it is an attachment catalog entry for an image asset, and that is exactly what the file contains.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register and timestamp an image asset attachment so the design-assets channel memory can reference and track the Price_Icon resource.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to target a design-assets image resource.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel attachment identifier and a specific design-assets image resource, enabling asset tracking and retrieval.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record that maps attachment att-349f889b in the design-assets channel to the image target image://design-assets/icons/Price_Icon with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to catalog an image asset attachment for retrieval and tracking within the design-assets channel memory.
